item.title: The MOST "Unsordid Act" in History?

#history #ww2 #us #uk

item.title: Andrew goes to Palestine
item.author: Channel 5 with Andrew Callaghan

item.title: Illegitimate children in Iran - Invisible and without rights | DW Documentary
item.author: DW Documentary
item.content:encoded:
Leyla loves her daughter Nila more than anything. But there’s a problem: according to Iranian law, Nila does not exist. Because she is an illegitimate child, she has no papers or rights. Mother and daughter must fight the absurd rules of a disingenuous regime.
But the resourceful duo refuse to give up. They hope to become part of Iranian society and save Nila from a life in hiding. "Nila's Dream in the Garden of Eden" accompanies Leyla in her struggle to achieve recognition of her daughter Nila's identity. Until then, she cannot obtain papers, go to school or receive medical care. The doors of Iranian society are closed to her.
Leyla and Nila desperately try to escape this cycle and free themselves from life in the shadows. They fight against bureaucratic hurdles and the opaque rules of an arbitrary state that is blind to the fate of a child. Despite everything, they manage to remain optimistic - they paint, sing, dance and fantasize about great journeys, a jet-set life of luxury and a miraculous twist of fate. They are a living example of how dreams and hopes can defy even the bleakest circumstances.
Film director Niloufar Taghizadeh knows Leyla from her school days. Years later, she learns of Leyla’s story, and a fate shaped by an absurd loophole in Iranian law. Driven by the desire to change the lives of the two, as well as that of many other undocumented children in Iran, she embarks on a risky journey to make this film. For almost three years, she accompanies the pair and films, partly undercover, in Iranian government offices and behind closed doors with decision-makers in the system to tell a story that, officially at least, doesn’t exist.
#documentary #dwdocumentary #dwdocs ______
DW Documentary gives you knowledge beyond the headlines. Watch top documentaries from German broadcasters and international production companies. Meet intriguing people, travel to distant lands, get a look behind the complexities of daily life and build a deeper understanding of current affairs and global events. item.title: The Truth about Space Data Centers
item.author: Real Engineering
item.content:encoded:

Nevin Spoljaric, Jason Clark, Thomas Barth, Johnny MacDonald, Stephen Foland, Alfred Holzheu, Abdulrahman Abdulaziz Binghaith, Brent Higgins, Dexter Appleberry, Alex Pavek, Marko Hirsch, Mikkel Johansen, Hibiyi Mori. Viktor Józsa, Ron Hochsprung

item.title: Brexit, 10 Years On: What It Actually Cost Britain
item.author: Patrick Boyle
item.content:encoded:
Protect your privacy on the cellular level with Cape at https://cape.co/patrickboyle0626 Use code PATRICKBOYLE33 at checkout for 33% off your first 6 months.
Ten years on from the 2016 Brexit referendum, the UK is about to appoint its seventh Prime Minister in a decade — so was Brexit worth it? In this video we break down the real economic impact of Brexit: how much it actually cost the UK economy, why the doom-laden Treasury forecasts and the Leave campaign's £350 million NHS bus were both wrong, and what the data really shows about GDP, trade, the City of London, and business investment. We dig into the goods-versus-services split, why the people who voted Leave were hit hardest while the metropolitan elites worked around it, the great post-Brexit immigration paradox and the "Boriswave," whether Britain is really "poorer than Mississippi," and why both Brexit and the new "Rejoin" campaign rely on the same magical thinking. Finally, we look at Brexit as a global turning point — the first crack in the post-Cold-War order — and ask why ten years of arguing about Europe distracted Britain from the domestic problems actually driving its economic stagnation. Featuring research from the NBER, the OBR, the Bank of England, The Economist, the Financial Times and The Atlantic.
Patrick's Books: Statistics For The Trading Floor: https://amzn.to/3eerLA0 Derivatives For The Trading Floor: https://amzn.to/3cjsyPF Corporate Finance: https://amzn.to/3fn3rvC
Ways To Support The Channel Patreon: https://www.patreon.com/PatrickBoyleOnFinance Buy Me a Coffee: https://www.buymeacoffee.com/patrickboyle
Visit our website: https://www.onfinance.org Follow Patrick on Twitter Here: https://bsky.app/profile/pboyle.bsky.social
Business Inquiries ➡️ sponsors@onfinance.org

item.title: Missing people in Mexico – in the shadow of the World Cup | DW Documentary
item.author: DW Documentary
item.content:encoded:
Yesennia Aracely has been missing since March 2022. She was last seen near Akron Stadium in Guadalajara, Jalisco, one of the host cities of the World Cup. Jalisco is also the state with the highest number of enforced disappearances. 1
Yesennia is one of more than 100,000 people missing in Mexico, though researchers and activists estimate the real number could be much higher. 2 Families of missing people and search groups have criticized the government for the lack of support and attention, while resources are being allocated to the World Cup.
#documentary #dwdocumentary #dwdocs ______
We kindly ask viewers to read and stick to the DW netiquette policy on our channel: https://p.dw.com/p/MF1G

item.title: Young men are turning on Trump. Here’s why. | America, Actually
item.author: Vox
item.content:encoded:
Young men are no longer happy with Donald Trump. His net approval rating among the group that famously helped propel him to the White House in 2024 has dropped by 56 points.
But is this shift real, or just noise? Vox’s Astead Herndon recently went to an event billed as an opportunity for the administration to reconnect with young men to find out: the UFC Freedom 250 event in Washington DC.
00:00 - Is Trump losing young men? 00:58 - Inside the White House’s UFC event 01:25 - Gas prices and the Iran war 03:15 - Epstein files and a crisis of elite transparency 09:01 - Where masculinity and politics overlap 12:43 - Jack Posobiec on how Trump reached young men in 2024 16:58 - Defending the Trump administration’s foreign policy 17:49 - The Epstein dilemma 23:07 - Can JD Vance woo young men? 24:01 - The Blue Dog strategy 26:40 - What Astead took away from his trip to Washington DC
America, Actually publishes video episodes every Saturday tackling key issues in politics, culture, and the economy. Subscribe to Vox’s YouTube channel to get them. Listen to episodes of America, Actually on Apple Podcasts, Spotify, or your favorite app.
If you enjoy our reporting and want to hear more from Vox journalists, sign up for our Patreon at patreon.com/vox. Each month, our members get access to exclusive videos, livestreams, and chats with our newsroom.

item.title: Why Trump is losing young men
item.author: Vox
item.content:encoded:
Young men aren’t as sure about President Donald Trump as they once were. Polling indicates that men under 30 now have a disapproval rating of Trump that’s over 50 percentage points. Vox’s Astead Herndon went to Washington, D.C. to talk to young men attending the UFC fight on the White House lawn and see what concerns they have about the president.
Listen to the full episode of America, Actually wherever you get your podcasts or watch here on YouTube.

I'm Thomas and I do videos and reviews on mechanical keyboards ranging from the most sickening modern RGB gaming keyboards to vintage hardware relics, or sometimes keycaps or keyswitches ranging from Cherry MX to Alps SKCM to IBM buckling springs and anything in between.
Follow me on Twitter for updates on my keyboard videos! https://twitter.com/chyrosran22
The practice sentence was: "Hello my name is Thomas and I'm typing on a Modelflabs B104 round 2 keyboard right now. This is an absolute joy to type on, and it looks beautiful! I just wish it was a little bit smoother."

item.title: Former adviser on Trump’s economic worldview | FRONTLINE
item.author: FRONTLINE PBS | Official
item.content:encoded:
Stephen Moore was an economic adviser to President Donald Trump. He is an economist, columnist and television commentator. He previously held positions at the conservative think-tanks The Heritage Foundation and the Cato Institute.
This journalism is made possible by viewers like you. Donate to FRONTLINE now: https://bit.ly/47DFzCb
And support your local PBS station here: https://www.pbs.org/donate
Moore spoke to FRONTLINE’s James Jacoby on April 2, 2026, for our documentary "The President vs. the Fed." Watch excerpts from that interview above.

item.title: How a lost giraffe revealed the dark side of exotic animal ranches in the US
item.author: Vox
item.content:encoded:
Gracie the giraffe escaped from a Texas ranch and became a viral animal story. But her escape points to a much bigger question: why are giraffes being bred, sold, and kept privately in the US?
Vox senior reporter Kenny Torrella looks into the exotic animal breeding, private ownership, roadside zoos, and hunting operations behind the viral moment.
Read the full story on Vox.com.
Subscribe to our channel and turn on notifications (🔔) so you don't miss any videos: http://goo.gl/0bsAjO

#castles #medievalcastle #medievalhistory

item.title: Brett Goldstein's was NOT Ready for this Audition | The Drawing Room with Mo Welch
item.author: PBS
item.content:encoded:
When your interview subject is also your friend, something shifts. Brett Goldstein — co-creator of Shrinking, Emmy Award winner for Ted Lasso, and Mo Welch's actual real-life friend — arrives at The Drawing Room after a long day of filming, as ever the polite gentleman. What follows is less an interview than a reunion: two people who have traveled and had adventures together, now sitting across easels and pretending to focus on their portraits.
The conversation begins with the most urgent question: why two T's in Brett? From there it moves to the before times — the grimy London open mics, dying onstage at a place called The Lion's Den, sitting in the back with friends writing jokes and laughing about how badly it went. Brett misses it. Mo misses it too. For a moment, the studio might as well be that back room.
Then it’s the Mamma Mia! story. Specifically: how a young Brett Goldstein showed up to a dance audition in jeans, survived several elimination rounds by hiding at the back of a crowd of 100 leotard-clad dancers, and — when the choreographer finally came close enough to notice him — jumped over his own leg and hoped for the best. He did not get the part. He does, however, still remember the body ripple. Brett and Mo also talk about craft. Brett describes how he sees the world: "funny and embarrassing and awful and tragic and depressing and brilliant." He doesn't separate those things. It all comes out together, and that, he says, is why his work lands the way it does. Mo, who mentions she completed therapy ("they gave me my certificate"), asks Brett to diagnose her anyway. His verdict: she works too hard and loves too hard. She seems to accept this.
There are quick draws — Michelle Pfeiffer and Wario, respectively — movies that make them cry, and a running disagreement about whether Brett's portrait of Mo looks like a person or an apple. When they finally reveal their sketches, the friendship is right there on the paper — imperfect, honest, and genuinely funny.
Made possible by viewers like you. Support your local PBS station: https://www.pbs.org/donate
Subscribe to the PBS channel for more clips: https://www.youtube.com/@PBS
Enjoy full episodes of your favorite PBS shows anytime, anywhere with the free PBS app: https://to.pbs.org/2QbtzhR
FOLLOW PBS:
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/PBS/ X: https://twitter.com/PBS/ Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/PBS/ TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@pbs Threads: https://www.threads.net/@pbs
The Drawing Room with Mo Welch What happens when interviews become art projects? Comedian and illustrator Mo Welch invites notable guests to join her at the easel, turning conversations into creative collaborations. This non-traditional PBS series puts Mo’s full talents on display, blending humor, vulnerability, and artistry as she and her guest draw each other out—literally and figuratively.

item.title: Hitler’s invasion of the Soviet Union: Operation Barbarossa (2/2) | DW Documentary
item.author: DW Documentary
item.content:encoded:
In early September 1941, the German Wehrmacht was advancing on all fronts in the Soviet Union. Kyiv had fallen, the Smolensk stronghold had been defeated and Leningrad was under siege. Hitler intended to launch a major offensive against Moscow before the winter.
Under the code name Operation Typhoon, the ‘final battle’ began - a desperate fight for the Soviet capital. The Vyazma-Bryansk double battle was aimed at crushing Red Army units around Moscow before conquering the city itself. Initially, the Wehrmacht managed to encircle and wear down the Soviet defenders. However, rain and mud then brought the German advance to a standstill. When frost set in after a good two weeks, troops were back on the move and the battle for Moscow could begin.
Thousands of civilians were drafted in to bolster defenses around the metropolis. Then Stalin ordered the evacuation of the city on 16 October. Panic and looting ensued. Three days later, a state of siege was declared and martial law was imposed. Despite major supply problems, a new German offensive was launched in mid-November, which ultimately failed.
By the spring of 1942, Soviet troops were able to regain large parts of territory lost to the Germans. The German defeat at Stalingrad in 1943 is also regarded as a decisive turning point in the conflict. Thereafter, the German front never recovered and entered a period of inexorable decline. Despite a desperate propaganda push, including a speech by Hitler’s propaganda minister Joseph Goebbels calling for ‘total war’, the German people had started to lose faith and doubt their nation’s victory prospects. Operation Barbarossa failed and ultimately led to Germany's unconditional surrender in May 1945.
Part 1: https://youtu.be/4FS34HAYgdo
#documentary #dwdocumentary #dwdocs ______

item.title: Jennifer Doudna Shares Her Pandemic Nobel Story
item.author: Bloomberg Originals
item.content:encoded:

item.title: An industrial mystery in Brooklyn’s Greenpoint neighborhood
item.author: Vox
item.content:encoded:
How can a company make 200,000 of something and become world-renowned, yet still go out of business?
This is Pin Point, the series where Vox producer Nate Krieger throws a dart at a map and investigates the surprising history of whatever address he hits. In this episode, he investigates an address where the most famous anvils in the world were made — until all of a sudden they weren’t.
Subscribe to our channel and turn on notifications (🔔) so you don't miss any videos: http://goo.gl/0bsAjO

item.title: The Strangest Medical Stories We Could Find | Compilation
item.author: Weird History
item.content:encoded:
The History of Medicine is a wild one. How humans figured out cures and even developed surgical procedures that saved lives! Often times what seemed to work in the past was later found to be detrimental to health and then abandoned. Today on Weird History we go into various aspects of the history of Medicine, the good, the bad, and the out-right miraculous! What is your favorite part about the history of all things medical? Let us know in the comments!
Be sure to subscribe to the Weird History Newsletter: https://bit.ly/WeirdHistoryNews
Chapters: 00:00:00 - What It Was Like Going To A Doctor In Wild West 00:10:11 - What It's Like to Be In an Iron Lung 00:21:34 - Bizarre and Bloody Practices of Medieval Barbers 00:33:22 - A Doctor Tried to Resurrect George Washington From the Dead 00:45:52 - What It's Actually Like To Have A Lobotomy 00:57:04 - The Crazy True Story Of The First Plastic Surgery In History 01:07:13 - Walter Jackson Freeman II The Champion Of The Lobotomy 01:22:33 - Horrifying Things We Used To Use As Medicine
#medicine #compilation #weirdhistory

item.title: Why do these metronomes sync up?
item.author: Veritasium
item.content:encoded:
Watch what happens when you start a metronome ticking on an unstable platform, out of sync with two others.
Slowly over time, they all sync up.
How does this happen?

item.title: The internet is all bots now | The Gray Area
item.author: Vox
item.content:encoded:
Sean talks with Atlantic writer Charlie Warzel about the increasingly weird experience of being online. They discuss AI-generated content, bots, algorithms, the “dead internet theory,” and why so much of the web now feels artificial, manipulated, or unreal. They also explore psyops, conspiracy culture, social media, and the deeper question lurking beneath the AI boom: What are human beings actually for?
Subscribe to our channel! http://goo.gl/0bsAjO
Host: Sean Illing (@seanilling) Guest: Charlie Warzel (@cwarzel)
00:00 Intro 01:45 The bot-haunted internet 08:53 Why the internet feels fake 15:11 The post-human internet 22:37 What is a psyop? 29:22 What is AI slop? 37:57 Charlie Warzel’s favorite part of the internet
We would love to hear from you. To tell us what you thought of this episode, email us at thegrayarea@vox.com or leave us a voicemail at 1-800-214-5749. Your comments and questions help us make a better show.
And you can watch new episodes of The Gray Area on YouTube. New episodes drop every Monday and Friday.

item.title: Why South Korea’s AI Stock Mania Is a Warning to the World
item.author: Bloomberg Originals
item.content:encoded:
South Korea’s stock market has surged about 200% year-on-year, powered by retail investors chasing an artificial intelligence-fueled chip rally. But with extreme volatility and a heavy reliance on two companies, is this a sustainable boom or a bubble waiting to burst?
For more on what's happening in stocks, bonds, currencies and commodities and what will move them next, subscribe here to Bloomberg Markets Daily. https://www.bloomberg.com/account/newsletters/markets-daily
00:00 Introduction 02:03 AI investment 03:10 Korea’s AI chip earnings 03:34 Meet a retail investor 04:30 YouTube finfluencers 05:30 Leveraged ETFs 07:00 Kospi concentration
#SouthKorea #Markets #KOSPI
-------- Like this video? Subscribe: http://www.youtube.com/Bloomberg?sub_confirmation=1

item.title: How Much Does Air Weigh?
item.author: StarTalk
item.content:encoded:
Order Take Me to Your Leader here 👇 https://www.simonandschuster.com/books/Take-Me-to-Your-Leader/Neil-deGrasse-Tyson/9781668249970
How does air pressure work? Neil deGrasse Tyson and Chuck Nice break down measuring air pressure and why champagne doesn’t pop in a submarine.
Timestamps: 00:00 - Introduction: Air Pressure 00:23 - Measuring Atmospheric Pressure 04:02 - Water Boiling 05:16 - Force Over an Area 08:16 - Suction Cups 11:00 - The Speed of Pressure 11:36 - Champagne Under the Sea
Check out our second channel, @StarTalkPlus

#StarTalk #NeildeGrasseTyson

item.title: Andrew detained by Israeli forces in Palestine
item.author: Channel 5 with Andrew Callaghan

item.title: A new organ donation method is changing transplants in the US
item.author: Vox
item.content:encoded:
This type of organ donation barely existed in the U.S. not that long ago.
Today, it's helping make thousands more kidney, liver, heart, and lung transplants possible. But as it's expanded, it's also raised difficult questions about patient protections, medical ethics, and public trust.
This is the story of donation after circulatory death (DCD — how it changed organ transplantation, and why some experts say the safeguards haven't evolved as quickly as the practice itself.
Watch the full breakdown above. 👆
Subscribe to our channel and turn on notifications (🔔) so you don't miss any videos: http://goo.gl/0bsAjO
Vox.com is a news website that helps you cut through the noise and understand what's really driving the events in the headlines. Check out http://www.vox.com.
Watch our full video catalog: http://goo.gl/IZONyE Follow Vox on TikTok: http://tiktok.com/@voxdotcom Check out our articles: https://www.vox.com/ Listen to our podcasts: https://www.vox.com/podcasts

item.title: How Elite French Winemakers Outsmarted The Nazi's During WW2
item.author: Timeline - World History Documentaries
item.content:encoded:
Between 1940 and 1945, France experienced its darkest hour under Nazi occupation. But while a few resisted, elite luxury dynasties in Bordeaux and Champagne discovered a mouthwatering business opportunity. This premium documentary exposes how France’s wealthiest wine merchants built colossal fortunes trading with the German army, acquired confiscated Jewish estates, and successfully paid off postwar authorities to hush up their crimes. Eighty years later, the wall of silence remains unbroken. Discover the shocking history of the Nazi Wine Führers and the secret collaboration that the world’s most prestigious brands are still desperate to hide.
You can now become a History Hit member right here on YouTube! Join for access to a new exclusive documentary every week, and access to over 160+ of our documentaries presented by world renowned historians like Dan Snow, Eleanor Janega, Tristan Hughes, Mary Beard, Matt Lewis and more. Get an exclusive release every week by signing up here: https://bit.ly/4pyExyn
This channel is part of the History Hit Network. Any queries, please contact owned-enquiries@littledotstudios.com

item.title: Thirsty Wallaby Picks the Wrong Spot | The Kimberley: Australia's Wild West
item.author: PBS
item.content:encoded:
Watch more from this series: https://to.pbs.org/4vQBvHM At first light, hundreds of agile wallabies make their way down to the river’s edge to get some water during the dry season. What awaits them in the river is the largest and most aggressive reptile on Earth: the saltwater crocodile. Watch as wallabies risk it all for water while avoiding the saltwater crocodile that patrols the river.
This program is made possible by viewers like you. Support your local PBS station: https://www.pbs.org/donate
Subscribe to the PBS channel for more clips: https://www.youtube.com/@PBS
Enjoy full episodes of your favorite PBS shows anytime, anywhere with the free PBS app: https://to.pbs.org/2QbtzhR
FOLLOW US:
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/PBS/ X: https://twitter.com/PBS/ Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/PBS/ TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@pbs Threads: https://www.threads.net/@pbs
#australia #wallabies #crocodile
The Kimberley: Australia’s Wild West This three-part series captures never-before-seen footage of The Kimberley, Australia’s remote northwest region, from a First Nations perspective, chronicling a year of extreme tropical seasons to reveal the stories hidden within the rugged landscape.
The Kimberley spans over 400,000 square kilometers of tropical wilderness, including turquoise coastlines, towering gorges, sandy deserts and sprawling savanna. The harsh, inaccessible terrain hides remarkable biodiversity, from the fearsome crocodiles who rule the rivers, to the marsupial gliders who soar over the savanna, to the hundreds of thousands of migratory shorebirds who travel from all over the planet to feed on the rich coastline. This near-pristine corner of the world is vital refuge for countless creatures. It is also the frontline of biodiversity conservation, where Traditional Owners and ecologists are working together to save the species who call the Kimberley home.
Watch more from this series: https://to.pbs.org/4vQBvHM

item.title: Inside the Engineering Project That Rewrote Earth's Geography | BBC Earth Science
item.author: BBC Earth Science
item.content:encoded:
Civilisations have always built near water, but China decided to move the water to the civilisation. The Shahe Aqueduct stands as one of humanity’s most daring engineering triumphs: a multi-kilometre river in the sky.
Best of Earth Science: http://bit.ly/EarthLabOriginals Best of BBC Earth: http://bit.ly/TheBestOfBBCEarthVideos
This is a channel from BBC Studios who help fund new BBC programmes. Service information and feedback: http://bbcworldwide.com/vod-feedback--contact-details.aspx

item.title: Canada's multicultural metropolis: When night falls in Toronto | DW Documentary
item.author: DW Documentary
item.content:encoded:
"We're just like New York," they say of their city, and usually add: "Only nicer!" Toronto is a melting pot of diverse cultures. Most of its residents have immigrated, at some point.
The documentary features the Neon Riders of Toronto, decorating their bikes with colorful lights and heading out at night for a ride and a party. Then there’s 28-year-old Jill, who opts to spend the night catching raccoons instead of losing herself on a club dancefloor. Or Quinn, who upholds the traditions of his Indigenous ancestors through painted constellations. And the guys from the Latino band ‘6ix Pal Mundo’, who are creating a new soundtrack for the city. These are just a few of the many stories and moments in a film about a city that welcomes everyone, is beautiful but never pretentious, and doesn’t beg to be loved -- but is adored by its inhabitants. A place where entering the world’s largest underground pedestrian tunnel system can feel like taking a journey through several different continents.
Canada’s largest city lives up to its promise of being a thriving multicultural metropolis. Seven million people from over 230 nations live in the Greater Toronto Area. As most residents will tell you with pride, if you make the most of your opportunities, you can achieve anything here. One of the reasons why Toronto is arguably the fastest-growing city in North America. But that brings another set of problems: the number of homeless people has doubled within four years, to more than 15,000.
#documentary #dwdocumentary #dwdocs #canada ______
DW Documentary gives you knowledge beyond the headlines. Watch top documentaries from German broadcasters and international production companies. Meet intriguing people, travel to distant lands, get a look behind the complexities of daily life and build a deeper understanding of current affairs and global events. item.title: "Good luck." Jennifer Doudna on AI Drug Discovery Promises
item.author: Bloomberg Originals
item.content:encoded:
Will AI cure cancer? Nobel laureate Jennifer Doudna discusses the hype around AI in medicine and why scientific breakthroughs will still require human ingenuity and a lot of hard work.
Watch more on The Circuit with Emily Chang https://www.bloomberg.com/news/videos/2026-06-24/the-future-of-gene-editing-with-jennifer-doudna-video?srnd=phx-markets

item.title: Behind the Scenes of a Major Museum Exhibition
item.author: Royal Armouries
item.content:encoded:
Ever wondered what goes into a major international exhibition? Our new Genghis Khan exhibit is the result of months of hard work from across the museum, including preventative conservation.
Sian Blake, Preventative Conservator, takes us through the process 👩‍🔬
Get your tickets now via our website, link on our page 🔗
#museum #exhibition #genghiskhan #conservation

item.title: How a broken heart affects your body - Roni Shanoada
item.author: TED-Ed
item.content:encoded:
Dig into broken heart syndrome, and find out how grief and traumatic events can affect your body and health.
--
Losing a loved one can be one of the most traumatic experiences of a person’s life. Grief brings on a whole host of strong emotions that impact both our mental and physical health. But is it possible for these powerful feelings to endanger your life? In other words, can you actually die from a broken heart? Roni Shanoada explores how traumatic events can affect your body.
Lesson by Roni Shanoada, directed by Joana Sus, NOMINT.
Support Our Non-Profit Mission ---------------------------------------------- Support us on Patreon: http://bit.ly/TEDEdPatreon Check out our merch: http://bit.ly/TEDEDShop ----------------------------------------------
Connect With Us ---------------------------------------------- Sign up for our newsletter: http://bit.ly/TEDEdNewsletter Follow us on Facebook: http://bit.ly/TEDEdFacebook Find us on Twitter: http://bit.ly/TEDEdTwitter Peep us on Instagram: http://bit.ly/TEDEdInstagram ----------------------------------------------
Keep Learning ---------------------------------------------- View full lesson: https://ed.ted.com/lessons/how-a-broken-heart-affects-your-body-roni-shanoada Dig deeper with additional resources: https://ed.ted.com/lessons/how-a-broken-heart-affects-your-body-roni-shanoada/digdeeper
Learn more Marjane Satrapi: https://bit.ly/4xRMMJZ Check out the graphic novel “Persepolis”: https://bit.ly/PRHPersepolis
Animator's website: https://www.compote-collective.com/team ----------------------------------------------
Thank you so much to our patrons for your support! Without you this video would not be possible! Barbara Smalley, Megan Douglas, Tim Leistikow, Ka-Hei Law, Hiroshi Uchiyama, Mark Morris, Misaki Sato, EdoKun, SookKwan Loong, Bev Millar, Lex Azevedo, Michael Aquilina, Jason A Saslow, Yansong Li, Cristóbal Moenne, Dawn Jordan, Prasanth Mathialagan, Samuel Doerle, David Rosario, Dominik Kugelmann - they-them, Siamak Hajizadeh, Ryohky Araya, Mayank Kaul, Christophe Dessalles, Heather Slater, Sandra Tersluisen, Zhexi Shan, Bárbara Nazaré, Andrea Feliz, Victor E Karhel, Sydney Evans, Latora, Noel Situ, emily lam, Sid, Niccolò Frassetto, Mana, I'm here because of Knowledge Fight Facebook group., Linda Freedman, Edgardo Cuellar, Jaspar Carmichael-Jack, Michael Burton, VIVIANA A GARCIA BESNE, The Vernon's, Olha Bahatiuk, Jesús Bíquez Talayero, Chels Raknrl, Sai Pranavi Jonnalagadda, Stuart Rice, and Jing Chen.

item.title: Best Simple System for Now • Daniel Terhorst-North • GOTO 2025
item.author: GOTO Conferences
item.content:encoded:
This presentation was recorded at GOTO Copenhagen 2025. #GOTOcon #GOTOcph https://gotocph.com
Daniel Terhorst-North - Originator of Behavior Driven Development (BDD) & Principal at Dan North & Associates @daniel-terhorst-north
RESOURCES https://bsky.app/profile/tastapod.com https://www.linkedin.com/in/tastapod https://github.com/tastapod https://mastodon.social/@tastapod http://dannorth.net/blog https://goalwards.co
Links https://xkcd.com/974 https://adventofcode.com
ABSTRACT Software teams often face a false choice: move fast and accumulate technical debt, or build the perfect system and miss the deadline. But there may be a third path, the Best Simple System for Now (BSSN).
This session introduces a pragmatic yet principled approach to software design: systems that are as simple as possible but no simpler. You’ll learn how to design code that is robust, change-friendly, and fit for purpose, while avoiding both gold-plating and corner-cutting.
Through practical examples, we explore how to manage complexity, embrace uncertainty, and make sound decisions using concepts like Cost of Delay and RAROC. [...]
TIMECODES 00:00 Intro 03:44 Agenda 05:05 Defining the best simple system for now 26:17 Do not try this at home 34:41 Why no one else is doing this 40:09 And then what? 43:00 Outro
Download slides and read the full abstract here: https://gotocph.com/2025/sessions/3737
RECOMMENDED BOOKS Donald G. Reinertsen • The Principles of Product Development Flow • https://amzn.to/3hJ2Ye2 Eliyahu M. Goldratt • Beyond the Goal • https://amzn.to/3wDbAL1 Kent Beck, Fowler, John, William, Don & Gamma • Refactoring • https://amzn.to/3SFBYbN Kent Beck & Cynthia Andres • Extreme Programming Explained • https://amzn.to/3KeL5P5 Jez Humble & Dave Farley • Continuous Delivery • https://amzn.to/3ocIHwd Robert B Cialdini • Influence, New and Expanded • https://amzn.to/3tL8GxB Aino Vonge Corry • Retrospective Antipatterns • https://amzn.to/3naFk84 Subramaniam & Hunt • Practices of an Agile Developer • https://amzn.to/2XjbWor
https://bsky.app/profile/gotocon.com https://www.linkedin.com/company/goto- https://www.instagram.com/goto_con https://www.facebook.com/GOTOConferences #Programming #SoftwareEngineering #Polyglot #Patterns #AntiPaterns #BuildTheRightProduct #GoodProgrammers #Change #CUPID #VESPA #Team #TechTeam #TodayInTech #DanNorth #DanielTerhorstNorth #GOTO
CHANNEL MEMBERSHIP BONUS Join this channel to get early access to videos & other perks: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCs_tLP3AiwYKwdUHpltJPuA/join
Looking for a unique learning experience? Attend the next GOTO conference near you! Get your ticket at https://gotopia.tech Sign up for updates and specials at https://gotopia.tech/newsletter
SUBSCRIBE TO OUR CHANNEL - new videos posted almost daily. https://www.youtube.com/user/GotoConferences/?sub_confirmation=1

item.title: Nikola Tesla - The visionary who invented our future | DW Documentary
item.author: DW Documentary
item.content:encoded:
Nikola Tesla, born in 1856 in what is now Croatia, was one of the world’s greatest inventors. He developed numerous innovations in the field of electrical engineering and was granted over 280 patents, many of them in the USA.
Nikola Tesla arrived in New York in 1884 with barely a cent to his name. He worked briefly for Thomas Alva Edison, the inventor of the light bulb. However, their collaboration soon ended in a dispute. In the years that followed, Tesla developed groundbreaking technologies and implemented alternating current systems for the Westinghouse company. These shaped modern electrification.
For decades, Tesla conducted research into high-frequency alternating currents and the vision of wireless power transmission - a concept that was far ahead of its time. He also recognized early on that solar energy would become one of the most important sources of energy in the future. However, his visionary inventions were not fully realized during his lifetime.
Tesla’s life was marked by extremes: periods of abject poverty and professional setbacks alternated with an eccentric lifestyle that’s been heavily mythologized. In 1943, he died alone and destitute in a New York hotel.
*[This documentary has been reuploaded due to technical issues with the audio. We apologise for any inconvenience this may have caused.]*
#documentary #dwdocumentary #dwdocs #tesla #nikolatesla ______

item.title: The World Cup is showing the value of American soft power
item.author: Vox
item.content:encoded:
The World Cup tourist videos taking over your feed aren't just wholesome content. They're a reminder of something bigger: America’s soft power
Vox's Constance Grady breaks down what the US’s welcoming spirit says about soft power at a moment when the country’s global reputation is under serious strain.

item.title: Why Trump is losing his war against algae
item.author: Vox
item.content:encoded:
President Donald Trump’s over $14 million Reflection Pool renovation project has not exactly gone according to plan. The pool was supposed to be “American flag blue,” but instead turned algae bloom green. Vox’s Benji Jones explains what makes the Washington, DC monument the perfect place for algae.

item.title: Why Fascism breaks Democracy
item.author: Imperial War Museums
item.content:encoded:
This video takes a look at historical Fascist regimes of the past, and their paths to power.
00:00 - Introduction 01:07 - What is Fascism? 03:04 - Step 1: Exploiting Crisis and Establishing Legitimacy 05:08 - Step 2: Legal and Legislative Manipulation 07:35 - Step 3: Capturing Institutions 08:47 - Step 4: Propaganda and Control of Information 10:39 - Step 5: Social Regimentation and Coercion 12:04 - What Italy and Germany Had in Common 12:50 - Other Autocracies 13:31 - Conclusion
Explore and licence the film clips used in this video: https://film.iwmcollections.org.uk/collections/_YawR3GAq
Follow IWM on social media: x.com/I_W_M instagram.com/imperialwarmuseums facebook.com/iwm.london tiktok.com/@imperialwarmuseums
Attributions: Bundesarchiv, Bild 183-S38324 / CC-BY-SA 3.0 Bundesarchiv, Bild 102-12023 / Georg Pahl / CC-BY-SA 3.0

item.title: The Fall Of Babylon and Rise of Persia's Achaemenid Empire
item.author: History Hit
item.content:encoded:
Watch this podcast episode from our sister channel, The Ancients, all about the dramatic fall of Babylon.
Check out The Ancient podcast channel here: https://www.youtube.com/@TheAncientsPodcast
The year is 539 BC. The greatest city of the ancient world has fallen. Babylon has opened it's gates to a new conqueror: the mighty Persian king Cyrus the Great.
In this episode of The Ancients, Tristan Hughes is joined once again by friend of the show Professor Lloyd Llewellyn-Jones to uncover the dramatic fall of Babylon and the ascent of Persia’s Achaemenid empire. From the final decades of Babylonian rule to Cyrus’ blood-stained triumph and the city’s remarkable survival under a new regime, they discover how one of history’s most iconic capitals lost its crown - a seismic chapter in the tumultuous story of the Ancient Near East.
0:00 Introduction 0:56 Historical significance of Babylon's transition 1:43 Analysing the historical sources 3:12 What Babylon looked like in the mid-6th century BC 6:38 The rise of Nabonidus and his mother Adad-Guppi 13:40 Nabonidus' devotion to the moon god Sin 16:20 Belshazzar's regency and Nabonidus' exile 19:53 The advance of Cyrus the Great and the Persian army 23:24 The brutal sack of Opis and the death of Belshazzar 25:32 Cyrus enters Babylon and the Cyrus cylinder 32:51 Cambyses' appointment and Elamite influence 35:43 Life in Babylon under Persian rule 38:49 The biblical story of the writing on the wall 43:33 Babylon's enduring prestige and Alexander the Great 46:18 Conclusion and further reading
#PerisanEmpire #Babylon #AncientHistory
You can now become a History Hit member right here on YouTube! Join for access to a new exclusive documentary every week, and access to over 160+ of our documentaries presented by world-renowned historians like Dan Snow, Eleanor Janega, Tristan Hughes, Mary Beard, Matt Lewis and more.
Get an exclusive release every week by signing up here: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCZwU2G-KVl-P-O-B35chZOQ/join

item.title: Why the Mamdani-backed NY primary wins are not a fluke
item.author: Vox
item.content:encoded:
New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani endorsed three congressional candidates during the New York primaries — and they all won. Vox’s Astead Herndon explains how this shows a continued rise in the populist left.

item.title: This Moon Collision Did Something Crazy on Earth
item.author: Astrum
item.content:encoded:
What created Tycho crater? Get a 10% discount on your next VisiSolar purchase when you use code ASTRUM2026 or the following link: https://visisolar.com/discount/ASTRUM2026
▀▀▀▀▀▀
Our Moon is covered with scars, but one is more recognisable than any other: Tycho. Formed in a violent impact that sent debris flying toward Earth, this iconic crater holds clues to the Moon’s hidden secrets. In this video, we’re digging into the evidence to discover what collided with the Moon, what happened here on Earth, and why the timeline still puzzles scientists today.
▀▀▀▀▀▀
0:00 Tycho Crater 2:24 Central Mountain 4:45 Lava Tsunami 7:43 Aftermath 8:39 Landslide 10:59 Raining Glass 12:03 Seen From Earth 13:08 Raining Glass on Earth 13:57 How Old Is Tycho? 15:55 Fresh Crater 17:17 Earth-Moon Exchange
▀▀▀▀▀▀
To stay on top of space news, sign up to the Astrum newsletter: https://astrumspace.kit.com
A huge thanks to our Patreons who help make these videos possible. Sign-up here to support the channel: https://bit.ly/4aiJZNF
Astrum Displate Posters: https://displate.com/artist/astrumspace?art=5f04759ac338b Astrum Merch: https://astrum-shop.fourthwall.com/
Join us on the Astrum discord: https://discord.gg/TKw8Hpvtv8
▀▀▀▀▀▀
Astrum Podcast on Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/show/6jPRrbq3o3dpvBb173ZTKi?si=a90d3efe3b704c83
Astrum Earth: https://youtube.com/@AstrumEarth Astrum Extra: https://www.youtube.com/@astrumextra
Astrum Spanish: https://www.youtube.com/@astrumespanol Astrum Portuguese: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UChn_-OwvV63mr1yeUGvH-BQ
▀▀▀▀▀▀
References: “Tycho Crater on the Moon Labeled”, via nasa.gov https://astrumspace.info/tychocrater “Tycho Crater's Central Peak on the Moon”, via nasa.gov https://astrumspace.info/tychopeak “South Side of Tycho”, via im-ldi.com https://astrumspace.info/southtycho “Chemical Composition of Lunar Surface”, via nih.gov https://astrumspace.info/lunarcomp “Melt Distribution Map of Tycho”, via usra.edu https://astrumspace.info/tychomelt “Tycho Crater Ejecta”, via usra.edu https://astrumspace.info/tychoejecta “Apollo Moon Sample Reveals Lunar Landslide”, via nhm.ac.uk https://astrumspace.info/moonsample “New 225 m Diameter Crater”, via usra.edu https://astrumspace.info/newcrater “Terrestrial Impact Melts and Glasses”, via researchgate.net https://astrumspace.info/impactmelts “Dating the Tycho Crater Impact”, via science.lu.se https://astrumspace.info/datingtycho
▀▀▀▀▀▀
Credits: Writer: Jon McColgan Video Editor & Animator: Stefan Payne-Wardenaar Audio Editor: James Horsley Researcher: Shourya Shrivastava Script Editor: Damaris McColgan Thumbnail Designers: Peter Sheppard, Juane Dames Publishing Lead: Georgina Brenner Production Coordinator: Rose Unwin Edit Producer: Poppy Pinnock Head of Astrum: Jess Jordan Creator of Astrum: Alex McColgan
With special thanks to: NASA/ESO/ESA
#Astrum #Space #Tycho

item.title: Palestine
item.author: Channel 5 with Andrew Callaghan
item.content:encoded:
Go to https://ground.news/channel5 to stay fully informed with all sides of every story. Subscribe for 40% off the Vantage plan through my link.
To see the second installment of our Palestine documentary, our full interview with Louis Thoreau and much more, head to our Patreon at https://patreon.com/channel5

item.title: The Napoleonic Surgical Kit That Hid Secret Pistols with Firearms Experts Jonathan & Mark
item.author: Royal Armouries
item.content:encoded:
What appears to be an ordinary early 19th-century surgical kit hides a remarkable secret.
This week, in a crossover that could rival Avengers Doomsday, Jonathan is joined by TikTok sensation Senior Curator of Firearms Mark Murray-Flutter to examine a rare Napoleonic-era medical chest that conceals more that meets the eye..
00:00 Intro 01:28 Examining the Surgical Kit 06:42 The Hidden Drawer Revealed 09:21 Opium, Medical Supplies & Accessories 13:10 The Concealed Flintlock Pistols 15:55 Who Was Francis Moore? 19:47 Waterloo Connections & Napoleonic History 29:36 Why Hide Guns Inside a Surgical Kit? 31:27 Outro
Subscribe to our channel for more videos about arms and armour
Help us bring history to life by supporting us here: https://royalarmouries.org/support-us/donations/
Sign up to our museum membership scheme here: https://royalarmouries.org/support-us/membership/
⚔Website: https://royalarmouries.org/home ⚔Blog: https://royalarmouries.org/stories/ ⚔Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/RoyalArmouriesMuseum/ ⚔Twitter: https://twitter.com/Royal_Armouries ⚔ Instagram: http://instagram.com/royalarmouriesmuseum
We are the Royal Armouries, the United Kingdom's national collection of arms and armour. Discover what goes on behind the scenes and watch our collection come to life. See combat demonstrations, experience jousting and meet our experts.
Have a question about arms and armour? Feel free to leave us a comment and we'll do our best to answer it.

item.title: The Ghosts Of Alcatraz
item.author: Weird History
item.content:encoded:
Alcatraz, also known as "The Rock," was a maximum-security fortress where only the most dangerous and clever criminals were sent. One of the most infamous prisons in American history, it sat alone on a rocky island in San Francisco Bay and was once considered escape-proof. But decades after the prison closed, questions still linger. Did some prisoners actually escape? What secrets remain hidden behind its crumbling walls? And could Alcatraz be a place where even death isn't enough to set you free?
In this episode of Weird History Presents: Graveyard Shift we take a look at the strange history of Alcatraz, the prison that may never let go.
Be sure to subscribe to the Weird History Newsletter: https://bit.ly/WeirdHistoryNews
#haunted #alcatraz #weirdhistory

item.title: Why AI Hasn't Cured Anything...Yet, According to Jennifer Doudna | The Circuit
item.author: Bloomberg Originals
item.content:encoded:
Emily Chang meets Nobel Prize-winning scientist and University of California, Berkeley, Professor Jennifer Doudna to discuss the origins of CRISPR, other breakthroughs in gene editing and what Silicon Valley gets wrong about the future of biology.
Watch more of The Circuit with Emily Chang: https://www.youtube.com/show/VLPLqq4LnWs3olV2nw7GgFjXHvxVc3T5hXXT?sbp=QAE%3D
Hosted by high-profile journalist Emily Chang, The Circuit is a fast-paced, dynamic series that lives at the intersection of culture, tech, entertainment, and business. Every week, Chang will go on location to meet the world’s most fascinating founders, influencers, and innovators, conducting intimate interviews and bringing audiences behind the scenes of the most impactful stories, launches, and trends.
#Tech #Science #AI #Biology #CRISPR
-------- Like this video? Subscribe: http://www.youtube.com/Bloomberg?sub_confirmation=1
Get unlimited access to Bloomberg.com for just $1.99 your first month: https://www.bloomberg.com/subscriptions?in_source=YoutubeOriginals Bloomberg Originals offers bold takes for curious minds on today’s biggest topics. Hosted by experts covering stories you haven’t seen and viewpoints you haven’t heard, you’ll discover cinematic, data-led shows that investigate the intersection of business and culture. Exploring every angle of climate change, technology, finance, sports and beyond, Bloomberg Originals is business as you’ve never seen it.
Subscribe for business news, but not as you've known it: exclusive interviews, fascinating profiles, data-driven analysis, and the latest in tech innovation from around the world.
Visit our partner channel Bloomberg News for global news and insight in an instant.

item.title: The Evolution of User Interfaces • Ken Pfeuffer • GOTO 2025
item.author: GOTO Conferences
item.content:encoded:
This presentation was recorded at GOTO Copenhagen 2025. #GOTOcon #GOTOcph https://gotocph.com
Ken Pfeuffer - The Mastermind Behind Apple's Gaze-and-Pinch Technology @kenpfeuffer3002
RESOURCES https://bsky.app/profile/kenpfeuffer.bsky.social https://twitter.com/KenPfeuffer https://www.linkedin.com/in/ken-pfeuffer-404377129 https://medium.com/@ken.pfeuffer https://kenpfeuffer.com
Links https://dl.acm.org/doi/epdf/10.1145/3131277.3132180 https://developer.apple.com/design/human-interface-guidelines/gestures#visionOS https://youtu.be/RVMf_SP7iTc https://cs.au.dk/news-events/ken-pfeuffers-research-comes-to-life-in-apples-vision-pro-headset
ABSTRACT The user interface—what connects you, me, and the digital world—has evolved remarkably from 2D GUIs with keyboards and mice to multi-touch, wearables, and now spatial computing (AR/VR/XR), reaching billions of people worldwide. Major shifts in interface paradigms are rare and often occur only once in decades, yet we may be on the cusp of one that could fundamentally transform everything we do with our hands on computers.
In this talk, I will trace this evolution and introduce Eye-Hand Symbiosis—my decade-long vision and research project exploring the fusion of eyes and hands for interaction. It is showing first signs of becoming reality, and I will share where this path might lead in the years ahead. [...]
TIMECODES 00:00 Intro 03:21 AR/VR today 08:50 Agenda 09:10 How did we get here? 20:22 Where does eye input come from? 38:01 What's ahead? 42:49 Outro
Download slides and read the full abstract here: https://gotocph.com/2025/sessions/3666
RECOMMENDED BOOKS Drew Reeves • X/UI Graphic Design For Beginners Made Simple • https://amzn.to/49t6Tmf Jenifer Tidwell, Charles Brewer & Aynne Valencia • Designing Interfaces • https://amzn.to/3L5HHKB LaViola Jr., Kruijff, Bowman, McMahan & Poupyrev • 3D User Interfaces • https://amzn.to/4aQVn6E
https://bsky.app/profile/gotocon.com https://twitter.com/GOTOcon https://www.linkedin.com/company/goto- https://www.instagram.com/goto_con https://www.facebook.com/GOTOConferences #UserInterface #UserExperience #GazePinch #UX #UI #DevEx #AR #VR #XR #AppleVisionPro #VisionOS #GazeComputerControl #EyeHandSymbiosis #Programming #SoftwareEngineering #KenPfeuffer #GOTO
CHANNEL MEMBERSHIP BONUS Join this channel to get early access to videos & other perks: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCs_tLP3AiwYKwdUHpltJPuA/join
Looking for a unique learning experience? Attend the next GOTO conference near you! Get your ticket at https://gotopia.tech Sign up for updates and specials at https://gotopia.tech/newsletter
SUBSCRIBE TO OUR CHANNEL - new videos posted almost daily. https://www.youtube.com/user/GotoConferences/?sub_confirmation=1

item.title: How Argentina NEARLY Destroyed a British Aircraft Carrier
item.author: Imperial War Museums
item.content:encoded: Argentine forces located the British carriers and prepared a potentially decisive air strike early in the conflict. But without enough wind for take-off the attack was cancelled, changing the course of the Falklands War.

item.title: For-Profit Adoptions & Misconduct Allegations (full documentary) | FRONTLINE (PBS) + Retro Report
item.author: FRONTLINE PBS | Official
item.content:encoded:
With Retro Report, an investigation into how so-called “baby brokers” have targeted pregnant women and families looking to adopt, and an epicenter of the problem in Utah.
This journalism is made possible by viewers like you. Donate to FRONTLINE now: https://bit.ly/47DFzCb
And support your local PBS station here: https://www.pbs.org/donate
The adoption industry in the U.S. is a multi-billion-dollar business, where demand outpaces the number of infants available for adoption. In “Baby Brokers,” FRONTLINE and Retro Report investigate a loosely-regulated corner of the industry described as “adoption tourism” and examine how allegations of misconduct involving for-profit agencies and brokers have mounted.
The documentary illuminates a patchwork system, where allegations of misconduct have stacked up in states like Utah with historically more permissive laws.
In the documentary, the head of an adoption agency in Utah says that out-of-state adoptions fulfill an important need. But other sources express concern about how brokers and agencies have used cash and housing offers as incentives for vulnerable women in other states.
In recent months, Utah has moved to tighten some of its laws in response to mounting criticisms from birth mothers, their advocates, and adoptive parents. And at the federal level, lawmakers from a number of states are backing a bipartisan bill aimed at reining in unlicensed brokers who funnel pregnant women and families across state lines for adoptions.
As the debate over “adoption tourism” continues, “Baby Brokers” is a must-watch exploration of the practice, the role of brokers, and the impact on both birth mothers and adoptive parents.
“Baby Brokers” is a FRONTLINE production with Retro Report. The correspondent and writer is Gabrielle Glaser. The director, writer and producer is Sarah Weiser. The senior producers are Frank Koughan and Bonnie Bertram. The executive producer of Retro Report is Kyra Darnton. The managing editor of FRONTLINE is Andrew Metz. The editor-in-chief and executive producer of FRONTLINE is Raney Aronson-Rath.
Explore additional reporting on “Baby Brokers” on our website: https://www.pbs.org/wgbh/frontline/documentary/baby-brokers/
#Documentary #Adoption #Utah
Subscribe on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/user/PBSfrontline Sign up for our newsletter: https://frontline.org/newsletter Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/frontlinepbs Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/frontline Bluesky: https://bsky.app/profile/frontlinepbs.bsky.social
FRONTLINE is produced at GBH in Boston and airs nationwide on PBS.
Funding for FRONTLINE is provided through the support of PBS viewers and by the Corporation for Public Broadcasting, with major support from Ford Foundation, and The Fialkow Family Foundation.
Additional support for FRONTLINE is provided by the Abrams Foundation, Park Foundation, John D. and Catherine T. MacArthur Foundation, Heising-Simons Foundation, and the FRONTLINE Trust, with major support from Jon and Jo Ann Hagler on behalf of the Jon L. Hagler Foundation, and Corey David Sauer, and additional support from Koo and Patricia Yuen.
Additional support for “Baby Brokers” was provided by the DJ McManus Foundation and the Jonathan Logan Family Foundation.
CHAPTERS: 00:00 - Prologue 01:05 - How Does ‘Adoption Tourism’ Work? 09:30 - Examining Lax Laws in the For-Profit Adoption Industry 13:33 - How Did Utah Become a Hotspot for Adoption? 20:06 - One Agency Head’s Perspective on Out-of-State Adoptions 24:13 - Critics’ Concerns About Out-of-State Adoptions 33:52 - How ‘Baby Brokers’ Targeted Pregnant Marshallese Women for ‘Adoption Tourism’ 48:04 - A Patchwork System of Laws Around Adoption 52:16 - Credits
